Bridge Inspection Report Brief Background: Eastern region Inspection The Karamoja sub-region consists of 8 districts. The bridges inspected during the discussion were located in Bududa, Bulambuli, Butaleja, Manafwa, Mbale, Pallisa, Sironko and Tororo districts. In particular, the bridges are listed below. . Date of Inspection: 30th  July 2005   - 6th August 2025

Bridge Inspection Report Brief Background: Eastern region Inspection The Karamoja sub-region consists of 8 districts. The bridges inspected during the discussion were located in Bududa, Bulambuli, Butaleja, Manafwa, Mbale, Pallisa, Sironko and Tororo districts. In particular, the bridges are listed below. . Rubbish dumped and temporary buildings built on the abutment walls. snippet: INTRODUCTION Bridges are an integral part of the road network and therefore require optimum management to preserve the road network. They are a key element with a high asset value and as such require efficient care to minimize losses. The failure of a bridge also poses a high safety risk to road users and highly disrupts traffic. As a result, attention must be given to the proper management of bridges and major culverts on the management. The first step to a proper management system is regular i

snippet: To solve the problem of not being able to add a photo to the Capturing Module or the BMS Hint: The Windows 7 64bit version saves files in different place. To know if your Windows 7 is a 32 bit or a 64 bit system, just look if you have a folder C:\Windows\SysWOW64 in your harddisk. If yes, then your system is a 64 bit system. for Windows XP, 2000 and Windows 7 32bit version: Download the Comdlg32.ocx file and save it to C:\Windows\System32 open a Command Prompt windows as Adminstrator:

snippet: WORK AROUND FOR COMPUTERS WHERE THE GIS VIEWER OF THE BMS DOES NOT INSTALL Sometimes the menu-buttons of the GIS are not enabled.  Only the Close button works. This is a workaround to install the GIS Viewer.  In the folder GIS_Install under the C:\Databases\BMS folder are a few files.  Run the following files in the following order: First VS2005_Dist_x86.exe

Bridge Inspection Report (KITGUM STATION)Brief BackgroundThe Kitgum station region is located in the Northern part of the country and consists of 3 districts; Kitgum, Agago and Pader. This area s transport network is characterized by mostly murram roads with small bridges that serve the simple but important purpose of easing navigation through this area that has several small rivers and swamps.  Date of Inspection: January 12, 2026Inspected By: Tayebwa MarkWalker KwesigaImmaculate KatutsiGidah NabatanziPrincipal Eng. Michael OcholaPrincipal Eng, Godwin TibaijukaPrincipal Eng.Gario Grace BagumaCorridor: Northern region Bridges Inspected:Bridge Bridge coordinates Coordinates ATUP BRIDGE Puranga Bridge   Corner Kilak   Pejule   Acholibur 32.9498 2.7376 AGORA BRIDGE Puranga Bridge   Corner Kilak   Pejule   Acholibur 32.9624 2.8214 PAGER BRIDGE Acholibur   Kitgum 32.8795 3.3012 ARINGA BRIDGE Kitgum   Madi Opei 33.0047 3.3966 AJAN BRIDGE Acholibur   Aswa 32.7674 3.0307 LAGWEL Kitgum Lukung Ngomoromo 32.8534 3.4409 ODYAKA Kitgum   Orom 33.0691 3.2603 AGAGO Patongo   Kalongo 33.3098 2.7797 AGAGO Patongo   Kalongo 33.3098 2.7799 AGAGO Patongo   Kalongo 33.3099 2.7801 AGAGO Patongo   Kalongo 33.3693 3.0077 BULUGI Kalongo   Kitgum  33.143 3.175 ODYAKA  Kalongo   Kitgum  33.062492 3.254043 LAGWEL Palabek   Padibe 32.8278 3.4661 OKWORA Padibe   Paloga 33.0555 3.6126 ARINGA Padibe   Paloga 32.9502 3.5926 ARINGA Potika   Lututur 32.8947 3.7167 LAKURE BRIDGE Potika   Lututur 32.9209 3.7304 KITGUM1. Atup Bridge (Puranga Bridge   Corner Kilak   Pejule   Acholibur)Bridge Type: Two span steel concrete composite slab bridge.Total Length: 8 metresDeck Width: 8 metres wide deck (including two 600mm wide raised parapets) Figure 1: Atup Bridge Approach and Perspective Views Key ObservationsErosion of bridge approach from ends.No gabion protectionRusting of the steel girdersBearing shelves are clogged with debrisConcrete damage at abutmentNo approach signage when approaching the bridge Figure 2: Approach eroding from ends of bridge   Figure 3: Damage at concrete abutment wing Figure 4: Rusted steel girder  Recommended MaintenanceIntervention Description Bridge approach renewal Excavate damaged section, backfill in layers, compact properly, or cast concrete slab. Embankment repair Remove loose soil, backfill eroded areas, compact, and re shape slope. Signage  Install posts in concrete bases and fix reflective signs at correct height. Gabion protection Place geotextile, assemble baskets, fill with rock, and secure lids Safety rail repainting Clean rusted surfaces, apply primer, and repaint with weather resistant paint. 2. Agora Bridge (Puranga Bridge   Corner Kilak   Pejule   Acholibur)Bridge Type: Single span solid slab bridgeTotal Length: 3 metresDeck Width: 5.15 metres wide deck (including two 450mm wide raised parapets) Figure 5: Agora Bridge Approach and Perspective Views Key ObservationsCracks observed on the interface of the abutment  wing wall likely due to differential settlement.No approach signage when approaching the bridgeVegetation overgrowth on the waterwayCorroded safety railings Figure 6: Crack at abutment wing interface Figure 7: Crack at abutment wing interfaceRecommended Maintenance   Cost EstimatesIntervention Description Repair the crack at the wing wall stem wall interface Seal and repair the cracks using epoxy motor Signage Placement of signage at bridge location. Clearing vegetation overgrowth Clearing and slashing Safety rail repainting Clean rusted surfaces, apply primer, and repaint with weather resistant paint. 3. Pager Bridge (Acholibur   Kitgum)Bridge Type: Three span concrete beam and slab bridgeTotal Length: 55 metresDeck Width: N Figure 8: Pager Bridge Approach and Perspective Views Key ObservationsExpansion joints have deteriorated beyond serviceable condition.Vegetation overgrowth along sidewalksVegetation overgrowth on the waterwaysGarbage mismanagement around abutment and bridgeBearing shelves clogged with debris at pier support Figure 9: Garbage mismanagement around bridge Figure 10: Debris in bearing shelf Figure 11: Vegetation overgrowth and debris along sidewalkRecommended Maintenance Intervention Description Expansion joint replacement Remove damaged joints, prepare seating area, and install new strip seal joints securely. Vegetation clearing Cut and remove overgrown vegetation along sidewalks and waterways; dispose off Proper garbage management Collect garbage around abutments, transport to approved disposal site, and set up routine cleaning schedule. Bearing shelf cleaning and debris removal Manually sweep shelves, use mechanical tools for stubborn debris, and ensure shelves are free of obstructions. 4. Aringa Bridge 1 (B156) (Kitgum   Madi Opei)Bridge Type: Two span concrete beam and slab bridgeTotal length: 37 metresDeck Width: N Figure 12: Aringa Bridge 1 Approach and Pers
